revision.comment: 「大津神社 (岐阜県飛騨市)」立ちあげました。補足等あれば宜しく。
revision.text:
'''大津神社'''は[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町]]にある[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、平安時代初期には祭祀が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。 [[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に高原郷の領主・江馬氏が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は戦国から江戸時代にかけて江馬氏〜三木氏〜金森氏（[[飛騨高山藩]]）に支配されたが、幕府の[[天領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（1804年）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。 維新直後の明治4年（1871年）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格。昭和 5年12月、内務省からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格。終戦後、昭和22年2月の[[神道指令]]と共に[[神社本庁]]に属して金幣社に列し、平成2年9月に特別金幣社に列した。
== 祭神 == *本社 :[[大彦命]] :武渟河別命（大彦命の子） :[[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（諏訪神社よりの合祀）
*摂社 :*神明神社 ::[[天照大神]] :*水波神社 ::水波能売大神 :*秋葉神社 ::火彦霊大神 :*大山祇神社 ::[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] :*墨縄神社 ::彦狭知大神 ::手置帆負大神 ::飛騨匠御霊 :*津島神社 ::[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] :*鉱山神社 ::[[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] ::[[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] :*菅原神社 ::[[菅原道真]] :*事代主神社 ::[[えびす|恵美須大神]] :*猿田彦神社 ::[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]]
*末社 :*稲荷神社 ::[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] :*木霊神社 ::久々能智大神
== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14〜16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている。
{{shinto-stub}} [[category:神社|おおつじんじゃ]] [[Category:府県社|おおつじんじゃ]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社|おおつじんじゃ]]

revision.comment: ページ [[大津神社 (岐阜県飛騨市)]] を [[大津神社 (飛騨市)]] へ移動: 曖昧さ回避は市町村名のみ

revision.comment: 神岡祭、その他について補足。
revision.text:
{{神社| 画像=[[画像:Ohtsu-Jinja Hida01.jpg|250px|大津神社・社殿(2006年)]]| 名称=大津神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市神岡町大字船津| 祭神=大彦命・武渟河別命・建南方富命| 社格=式内社| 例祭=4月第4土曜日}}
'''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]にある[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、平安時代初期には祭祀が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。 [[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に高原郷の領主・江馬氏が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は戦国から江戸時代にかけて江馬氏～三木氏～金森氏（[[飛騨高山藩]]）に支配されたが、幕府の[[天領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（1804年）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。 維新直後の明治4年（1871年）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格。昭和 5年12月、内務省からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格。終戦後、昭和22年2月の[[神道指令]]と共に[[神社本庁]]に属して金幣社に列し、平成2年9月に特別金幣社に列した。

*摂社 :*神明神社 ::[[天照大神]] :*水波神社 ::水波能売大神 :*秋葉神社 ::火彦霊大神 :*大山祇神社 ::[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] :*墨縄神社 ::彦狭知大神 ::手置帆負大神 ::飛騨匠御霊 :*津島神社 ::[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] :*鉱山神社 ::[[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] ::[[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] :*菅原神社 ::[[菅原道真]] :*事代主神社 ::[[えびす|恵美須大神]] :*猿田彦神社 ::[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]]
*末社 :*稲荷神社 ::[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] :*木霊神社 ::久々能智大神
== 神岡祭 == 神岡町にある大津神社・八幡宮・白山神社が同日に行う例祭の総称で、高山祭、古川祭とともに飛騨三大祭に数えられる。
== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14～16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている。
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:府県社|おおつしんしや]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社|おおつしんしや]]

== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14～16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている。
== 関連項目 == [[天満神社 (高山市)|天満神社]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:飛騨市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 画像=[[画像:Ohtsu-Jinja Hida01.jpg|250px|大津神社・社殿(2006年)]]| 名称=大津神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市神岡町大字船津| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|20|4.40|N|137|17|39.24|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=大彦命・武渟河別命・建南方富命| 社格=式内社、県社、特別金幣社| 例祭=4月第4土曜日}}
'''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]にある[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、平安時代初期には祭祀が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。
[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に[[高原郷]]の領主・江馬氏が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて江馬氏～三木氏～金森氏（[[飛騨高山藩]]）に支配されたが、幕府の[[天領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（[[1804年]]）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。
[[明治維新]]直後の[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格。[[昭和]]5年（[[1930年]]）12月、内務省からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格。終戦後、昭和22年（[[1947年]]）2月の[[神道指令]]と共に[[神社本庁]]に属して金幣社に列し、[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）9月に特別金幣社に列した。
== 祭神 == *本社 :[[大彦命]] :武渟河別命（大彦命の子） :[[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（諏訪神社よりの合祀）
*摂社 :*神明神社 ::[[天照大神]] :*水波神社 ::水波能売大神 :*秋葉神社 ::火彦霊大神 :*大山祇神社 ::[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] :*墨縄神社 ::彦狭知大神 ::手置帆負大神 ::飛騨匠御霊 :*津島神社 ::[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] :*鉱山神社 ::[[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] ::[[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] :*菅原神社 ::[[菅原道真]] :*事代主神社 ::[[えびす|恵美須大神]] :*猿田彦神社 ::[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]]
*末社 :*稲荷神社 ::[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] :*木霊神社 ::久々能智大神
== 神岡祭 == 神岡町にある大津神社・八幡宮・白山神社が同日に行う例祭の総称で、高山祭、古川祭とともに飛騨三大祭に数えられる。
== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14～16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}} </ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == [[天満神社 (高山市)|天満神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1252&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 大津神社 岐阜県神社庁公式ホームページ]

== 由緒 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、平安時代初期には祭祀が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。
[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に[[高原郷]]の領主・江馬氏が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて江馬氏、三木氏、金森氏（[[飛騨国|飛騨]][[飛騨高山藩|高山藩]]）にそれぞれ支配されたが、幕府の[[天領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（[[1804年]]）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。
[[明治維新]]直後の[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格。[[昭和]]5年（[[1930年]]）12月、内務省からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格。終戦後、昭和22年（[[1947年]]）2月の[[神道指令]]と共に[[神社本庁]]に属して金幣社に列し、[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）9月に特別金幣社に列した。
== 祭神 == *本社 *:[[大彦命]] *:武渟河別命（大彦命の子） *:[[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（諏訪神社よりの合祀） *摂社 *:*神明神社 *::[[天照大神]] *:*水波神社 *::水波能売大神 *:*秋葉神社 *::火彦霊大神 *:*大山祇神社 *::[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] *:*墨縄神社 *::彦狭知大神 *::手置帆負大神 *::飛騨匠御霊 *:*津島神社 *::[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] *:*鉱山神社 *::[[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] *::[[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] *:*菅原神社 *::[[菅原道真]] *:*事代主神社 *::[[えびす|恵美須大神]] *:*猿田彦神社 *::[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] *末社 *:*稲荷神社 *::[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] *:*木霊神社 *::久々能智大神
== 神岡祭 == 神岡町にある大津神社・八幡宮・白山神社が同日に行う例祭の総称で、高山祭、古川祭とともに飛騨三大祭に数えられる。
== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}} </ref>。

== 祭神 == {{Wikify|date=2020年4月|section=1}} <!-- 定義リストの入れ子が崩壊している。 -->
*本社 *:[[大彦命]] *:[[武渟川別|武渟河別命]]（大彦命の子） *:[[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（諏訪神社よりの合祀） *摂社 *:*神明神社 *::[[天照大神]] *:*水波神社 *::[[ミヅハノメ|水波能売大神]] *:*秋葉神社 *::[[火産霊|火彦霊大神]] *:*大山祇神社 *::[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] *:*墨縄神社 *::[[彦狭知命|彦狭知大神]] *::[[手置帆負命|手置帆負大神]] *::飛騨匠御霊 *:*津島神社 *::[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] *:*鉱山神社 *::[[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] *::[[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] *:*菅原神社 *::[[菅原道真]] *:*事代主神社 *::[[えびす|恵美須大神]] *:*猿田彦神社 *::[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] *末社 *:*稲荷神社 *::[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] *:*木霊神社 *::[[久久能智神|久々能智大神]]
== 神岡祭 == 神岡町にある大津神社・八幡宮・白山神社が同日に行う例祭の総称で、高山祭、古川祭とともに飛騨三大祭に数えられる。
== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}} </ref>。

[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に[[高原郷]]の領主・[[江馬氏]]が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて江馬氏、[[三木氏]]、[[金森氏]]（[[飛騨高山藩]]）にそれぞれ支配されたが、[[幕府領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（[[1804年]]）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。
[[明治維新]]直後の[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格。[[昭和]]5年（[[1930年]]）12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格。終戦後、昭和22年（[[1947年]]）2月の[[神道指令]]と共に[[神社本庁]]に属して[[金幣社]]に列し、[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）9月に特別金幣社に列した。
== 祭神 == {{Wikify|date=2020年4月|section=1}} <!-- 定義リストの入れ子が崩壊している。 -->
*本社 *:[[大彦命]] *:[[武渟川別|武渟河別命]]（大彦命の子） *:[[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（[[諏訪神社]]よりの合祀） *摂社 *:*神明神社 *::[[天照大神]] *:*水波神社 *::[[ミヅハノメ|水波能売大神]] *:*秋葉神社 *::[[火産霊|火彦霊大神]] *:*大山祇神社 *::[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] *:*墨縄神社 *::[[彦狭知命|彦狭知大神]] *::[[手置帆負命|手置帆負大神]] *::飛騨匠御霊 *:*津島神社 *::[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] *:*鉱山神社 *::[[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] *::[[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] *:*菅原神社 *::[[菅原道真]] *:*事代主神社 *::[[えびす|恵美須大神]] *:*猿田彦神社 *::[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] *末社 *:*稲荷神社 *::[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] *:*木霊神社 *::[[久久能智神|久々能智大神]]
== 神岡祭 == 神岡町にある[[大津神社]]・[[八幡宮]]・[[白山神社]]が同日に行う例祭の総称で、[[高山祭]]、[[古川祭]]とともに飛騨三大祭に数えられる。
== 船津盆踊り == 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、昭和44年（1969年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web |author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}} </ref>。

{{神社 | 名称 = 大津神社 | 画像 = [[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | 所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]大字船津 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 4.4 | 経度度 = 137 | 経度分 = 17 | 経度秒 = 39.4 | 祭神 = 大彦命・武渟河別命・建南方富命 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、県社、特別[[金幣社]] | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 4月第4土曜日 | 神事 = | 地図 = }} '''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。県社。特別[[金幣社]]。

== 歴史 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、[[平安時代|平安]]時代初期には[[祭|祭祀]]が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。
[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に[[高原郷]]の領主・[[江馬氏]]が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて江馬氏、[[三木氏]]、[[金森氏]]（[[飛騨高山藩]]）にそれぞれ支配されたが、[[幕府領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（1804年）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。
[[明治維新]]後の1871年（明治4年）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格された。1895年（明治28年）には735戸が焼失する船津大火が起こった。この大火では船津町役場、船津郵便局、銀行、[[円城寺 (飛騨市神岡町船津)|円城寺]]、大津神社なども焼失し、大津神社は現在地に移された。1916年（大正5年）9月には参道脇に劇場の大正座が開館した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975">三井金属鉱業修史委員会事務局『神岡鉱山写真史』三井金属鉱業、1975年</ref>。
1930年（昭和5年）12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格した。戦後の1947年（昭和22年）2月、[[神道指令]]とともに[[神社本庁]]に属して[[金幣社]]に列した。1948年（昭和23年）には大正座が船津劇場に改称した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/><ref>飛騨市教育委員会『神岡町史 写真編』飛騨市教育委員会、2010年</ref>。1970年代初頭に船津劇場が閉館すると、1973年（昭和48年）7月には劇場跡地に福祉会館が竣工した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/>。
1990年（平成2年）9月に特別金幣社に列した。文化5年（1808年）から約20年周期で式年大祭が行われている<ref>[https://hida.keizai.biz/headline/658/ 飛騨神岡・大津神社で「式年大祭」20年に一度、「映画予告みたいな」CMも話題に] 飛騨経済新聞、2014年5月2日</ref>。2014年（平成26年）には11回目の式年大祭が行われ、飛騨地方各地から約130社の神社と計約3500人の[[氏子]]が参集した。
== 境内 == * 社殿 * 本殿 * 摂宮 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 一の鳥居 - 1928年（昭和3年）竣工<ref>『写真アルバム 飛騨の昭和』樹林舎、2015年</ref>。 * 二の鳥居
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|摂末社 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|社務所 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|手水舎 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|一の鳥居 </gallery>
== 祭事 == === 神岡祭 === 神岡町にある大津神社、八幡宮、白山神社が同日に行う例祭の総称であり、[[高山祭]]、[[古川祭]]とともに「飛騨三大祭」に数えられる。
=== 船津盆踊り === 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、1969年（昭和44年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web |author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天満神社 (高山市)|天満神社]] * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Otsu-jinja (Hida)}} * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1252&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 大津神社] 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧県社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:飛騨市の歴史]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:飛騨市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 大津神社 | 画像 = [[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | 所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]大字船津 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 4.4 | 経度度 = 137 | 経度分 = 17 | 経度秒 = 39.4 | 祭神 = 大彦命・武渟河別命・建南方富命 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、県社、特別[[金幣社]] | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 4月第4土曜日 | 神事 = | 地図 = }} '''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。県社。特別[[金幣社]]。
== 祭神 == ; 本社 * [[大彦命]]（おおひこのみこと） * [[武渟川別|武渟河別命]]（たけぬなかわわけのみこと） - 大彦命の子。 * [[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） - [[諏訪神社]]から合祀。
{{表2列| ; 摂社 * 神明神社 ** [[天照大神]] * 水波神社 ** [[ミヅハノメ|水波能売大神]] * 秋葉神社 ** [[火産霊|火彦霊大神]] * 大山祇神社 ** [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] * 墨縄神社 ** [[彦狭知命|彦狭知大神]] ** [[手置帆負命|手置帆負大神]] ** 飛騨匠御霊 * 津島神社 ** [[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] * 鉱山神社 ** [[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] ** [[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] * 菅原神社 ** [[菅原道真]] ** 事代主神社 ** [[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] | ; 末社 * 稲荷神社 ** [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] * 木霊神社 ** [[久久能智神|久々能智大神]] }}
== 歴史 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、[[平安時代|平安]]時代初期には[[祭|祭祀]]が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。
[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に[[高原郷]]の領主・[[江馬氏]]が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて江馬氏、[[三木氏]]、[[金森氏]]（[[飛騨高山藩]]）にそれぞれ支配されたが、[[幕府領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（1804年）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。
[[明治維新]]後の1871年（明治4年）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格された。1895年（明治28年）には735戸が焼失する船津大火が起こった。この大火では船津町役場、船津郵便局、銀行、[[円城寺 (飛騨市神岡町船津)|円城寺]]、大津神社なども焼失し、大津神社は現在地に移された。1916年（大正5年）9月には参道脇に劇場の大正座が開館した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975">三井金属鉱業修史委員会事務局『神岡鉱山写真史』三井金属鉱業、1975年</ref>。
1930年（昭和5年）12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格した。戦後の1947年（昭和22年）2月、[[神道指令]]とともに[[神社本庁]]に属して[[金幣社]]に列した。1948年（昭和23年）には大正座が船津劇場に改称した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/><ref>飛騨市教育委員会『神岡町史 写真編』飛騨市教育委員会、2010年</ref>。1970年代初頭に船津劇場が閉館すると、1973年（昭和48年）7月には劇場跡地に福祉会館が竣工した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/>。
1990年（平成2年）9月に特別金幣社に列した。文化5年（1808年）から約20年周期で式年大祭が行われている<ref>[https://hida.keizai.biz/headline/658/ 飛騨神岡・大津神社で「式年大祭」20年に一度、「映画予告みたいな」CMも話題に] 飛騨経済新聞、2014年5月2日</ref>。2014年（平成26年）には11回目の式年大祭が行われ、飛騨地方各地から約130社の神社と計約3500人の[[氏子]]が参集した。
== 境内 == * 社殿 * 本殿 * 摂宮 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 一の鳥居 - 1928年（昭和3年）竣工<ref>『写真アルバム 飛騨の昭和』樹林舎、2015年</ref>。 * 二の鳥居
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|摂末社 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|社務所 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|手水舎 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|一の鳥居 </gallery>
== 祭事 == === 神岡祭 === 神岡町にある大津神社、八幡宮、白山神社が同日に行う例祭の総称であり、[[高山祭]]、[[古川祭]]とともに「飛騨三大祭」に数えられる。
=== 船津盆踊り === 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、1969年（昭和44年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天満神社 (高山市)|天満神社]] * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)|ja|天満神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Shrine | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

{{Shrine | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)|ja|天満神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Shrine | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)|ja|天満神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Otsu-jinja (Hida)}} * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1252&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Ōtsu Shrine] (Gifu Shrine Association)

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)|ja|天満神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Otsu-jinja (Hida)}} * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1252&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Ōtsu Shrine] (Gifu Shrine Association)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōtsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Hida City]], {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, Ōaza Funatsu | coordinates={{Coord|36|20|4.4|N|137|17|39.4|E}} | deity={{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}}, {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Prefectural Shrine, Special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | consecration_year=Unknown | festival=4th Saturday of April }}
'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町}}, [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Shrine * {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命}} * {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命}} - Son of Ōhiko no Mikoto. * {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}.
; Auxiliary Shrines * Shinmei Shrine ''' {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * Mizunami Shrine ''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神}} * Akiba Shrine ''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神}} * Ōyama-tsumi Shrine ''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山祇大神}} * Suminawa Shrine ''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神}} ''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神}} ''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman * Tsushima Shrine ''' {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男大神}} * Kōzan Shrine ''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神}} ''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神}} * Sugawara Shrine ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} ''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神}} ''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神}} * Sarutahiko Shrine ''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}}
; Subordinate Shrines * Inari Shrine ''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神}} * Kodama Shrine ''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神}}
== History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.
During the {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|ja|田中大秀}}.
In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} status.
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Auxiliary Shrines * Handwashing Basin * First Torii Gate (completed in 1928) * Second Torii Gate
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate </gallery>
== Festivals == === Kamioka Festival === The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|ja|八幡宮}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭}}.
=== Funatsu Bon Odori === Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

{{神社 | 名称 = 大津神社 | 画像 = [[File:Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (2).jpg|280px]] | 所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]大字船津 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 4.4 | 経度度 = 137 | 経度分 = 17 | 経度秒 = 39.4 | 祭神 = 大彦命・武渟河別命・建南方富命 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、県社、特別[[金幣社]] | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 4月第4土曜日 | 神事 = | 地図 = }} '''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]][[神岡町 (岐阜県)|神岡町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。県社。特別[[金幣社]]。
== 祭神 == ; 本社 * [[大彦命]]（おおひこのみこと） * [[武渟川別|武渟河別命]]（たけぬなかわわけのみこと） - 大彦命の子。 * [[建御名方神|建南方富命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） - [[諏訪神社]]から合祀。
{{表2列| ; 摂社 * 神明神社 ** [[天照大神]] * 水波神社 ** [[ミヅハノメ|水波能売大神]] * 秋葉神社 ** [[火産霊|火彦霊大神]] * 大山祇神社 ** [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]] * 墨縄神社 ** [[彦狭知命|彦狭知大神]] ** [[手置帆負命|手置帆負大神]] ** 飛騨匠御霊 * 津島神社 ** [[スサノオ|建速須佐之男大神]] * 鉱山神社 ** [[金山彦神|金山彦大神]] ** [[金山姫神|金山姫大神]] * 菅原神社 ** [[菅原道真]] ** 事代主神社 ** [[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 猿田彦神社 ** [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]] | ; 末社 * 稲荷神社 ** [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御霊大神]] * 木霊神社 ** [[久久能智神|久々能智大神]] }}
== 歴史 == 創始年代は不明。[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]・[[日本三代実録]]に依れば、[[平安時代|平安]]時代初期には[[祭|祭祀]]が行なわれていたとされ、また[[延喜式神名帳]]に飛騨國内八社の一つとして記されている。
[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間に[[高原郷]]の領主・[[江馬氏]]が武神信州[[諏訪大社|諏訪大明神]]の分霊を勧請、社号も大津神社から諏訪大明神と改めた。飛騨は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて江馬氏、[[三木氏]]、[[金森氏]]（[[飛騨高山藩]]）にそれぞれ支配されたが、[[幕府領]]となって約100年後の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（1804年）に[[田中大秀]]門下の稲田元浩・大森旭亭・吉村友閑斎らの考証により、社号を諏訪大明神から大津神社に復帰。
[[明治維新]]後の1871年（明治4年）10月、[[近代社格制度]]により[[筑摩県]]郷社に列格された。1895年（明治28年）には735戸が焼失する船津大火が起こった。この大火では船津町役場、船津郵便局、銀行、[[円城寺 (飛騨市神岡町船津)|円城寺]]、大津神社なども焼失し、大津神社は現在地に移された。1916年（大正5年）9月には参道脇に劇場の大正座が開館した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975">三井金属鉱業修史委員会事務局『神岡鉱山写真史』三井金属鉱業、1975年</ref>。
1930年（昭和5年）12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格した。戦後の1947年（昭和22年）2月、[[神道指令]]とともに[[神社本庁]]に属して[[金幣社]]に列した。1948年（昭和23年）には大正座が船津劇場に改称した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/><ref>飛騨市教育委員会『神岡町史 写真編』飛騨市教育委員会、2010年</ref>。1970年代初頭に船津劇場が閉館すると、1973年（昭和48年）7月には劇場跡地に福祉会館が竣工した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/>。
1990年（平成2年）9月に特別金幣社に列した。文化5年（1808年）から約20年周期で式年大祭が行われている<ref>[https://hida.keizai.biz/headline/658/ 飛騨神岡・大津神社で「式年大祭」20年に一度、「映画予告みたいな」CMも話題に] 飛騨経済新聞、2014年5月2日</ref>。2014年（平成26年）には11回目の式年大祭が行われ、飛騨地方各地から約130社の神社と計約3500人の[[氏子]]が参集した。
== 境内 == * 社殿 * 本殿 * 摂宮 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 一の鳥居 - 1928年（昭和3年）竣工<ref>『写真アルバム 飛騨の昭和』樹林舎、2015年</ref>。 * 二の鳥居
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|摂末社 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|社務所 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|手水舎 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|一の鳥居 </gallery>
== 祭事 == === 神岡祭 === 神岡町にある大津神社、[[朝浦八幡宮]]、[[白山神社 (飛騨市神岡町東町)|白山神社]]が同日に行う例祭の総称であり、[[高山祭]]、[[古川祭]]とともに「飛騨三大祭」に数えられる。
=== 船津盆踊り === 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、1969年（昭和44年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天満神社 (高山市)|天満神社]] * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Otsu-jinja (Hida)}} * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1252&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 大津神社] 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧県社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:飛騨市の歴史]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:飛騨市の建築物]]

1930年（昭和5年）12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]からの旨示達で[[岐阜県]]県社に昇格した。戦後の1947年（昭和22年）2月、[[神道指令]]とともに[[神社本庁]]に属して[[金幣社]]に列した。1948年（昭和23年）には大正座が船津劇場に改称した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/><ref>飛騨市教育委員会『神岡町史 写真編』飛騨市教育委員会、2010年</ref>。1970年代初頭に船津劇場が閉館すると、1973年（昭和48年）7月には劇場跡地に福祉会館が竣工した<ref name="三井金属鉱業1975"/>。
1990年（平成2年）9月に特別金幣社に列した。文化5年（1808年）から約20年周期で式年大祭が行われている<ref>[https://hida.keizai.biz/headline/658/ 飛騨神岡・大津神社で「式年大祭」20年に一度、「映画予告みたいな」CMも話題に] 飛騨経済新聞、2014年5月2日</ref>。2014年（平成26年）には11回目の式年大祭が行われ、飛騨地方各地から約130社の神社と計約3500人の[[氏子]]が参集した。
== 境内 == * 社殿 * 本殿 * 摂宮 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 一の鳥居 - 1928年（昭和3年）竣工<ref>『写真アルバム 飛騨の昭和』樹林舎、2015年</ref>。 * 二の鳥居
<gallery> Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|摂末社 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|社務所 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|手水舎 Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|一の鳥居 </gallery>
== 祭事 == === 神岡祭 === 神岡町にある大津神社、[[朝浦八幡宮]]、[[白山神社 (飛騨市神岡町東町)|白山神社]]が同日に行う例祭の総称であり、[[高山祭]]、[[古川祭]]とともに「飛騨三大祭」に数えられる。
=== 船津盆踊り === 毎年8月14日から16日に境内で行なわれるもので、1969年（昭和44年）に岐阜県の[[文化財|無形文化財]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=船津盆踊 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天満神社 (高山市)|天満神社]] * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Otsu-jinja (Hida)}} * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1252&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A7%E6%B4%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 大津神社] 岐阜県神社庁

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|qq=|1=Kamioka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine
* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

; Auxiliary Shrines
* Shinmei Shrine
''' [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* Mizunami Shrine
''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Akiba Shrine
''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi no Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Ōyama-tsumi Shrine
''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
* Suminawa Shrine
''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashiri no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teojihori no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman
* Tsushima Shrine
''' {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kōzan Shrine
''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135490520}}
''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135490521}}
* Sugawara Shrine
''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Sarutahiko Shrine
''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}

; Subordinate Shrines
* Inari Shrine
''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka no Mitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kodama Shrine
''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the [[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]] and the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|qq=|1=Ema Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|qq=|1=Miki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of [[:en:Tanaka Ōhide|Tanaka Ōhide]].

In 1871, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

<gallery>
Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (3).jpg|Auxiliary Shrines
Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (5).jpg|Administrative Office
Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (4).jpg|Handwashing Basin
Otsu-jinja Kamioka ac (6).jpg|First Torii Gate
</gallery>

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)]], and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|qq=|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the [[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]] and the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|qq=|1=Ema Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|qq=|1=Miki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of [[:en:Tanaka Ōhide|Tanaka Ōhide]].

In 1871, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)]], and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|qq=|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|qq=|1=Kamioka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the [[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]] and the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|qq=|1=Ema Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|qq=|1=Miki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of [[:en:Tanaka Ōhide|Tanaka Ōhide]].

In 1871, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Kamioka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the [[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]] and the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Ema Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Miki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of [[:en:Tanaka Ōhide|Tanaka Ōhide]].

In 1871, under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)]], and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

It is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]] in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.
