== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine
* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takeminakata}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

; Auxiliary Shrines

''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. '''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine
* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takeminakata}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. '''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine
* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takeminakata}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

; Auxiliary Shrines
* Shinmei Shrine
''' {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* Mizunami Shrine
''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Akiba Shrine
''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Ōyama-tsumi Shrine
''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
* Suminawa Shrine
''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teojihori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman
* Tsushima Shrine
''' {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kōzan Shrine
''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490520|1=Kanayamahiko}}
''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490521|1=Kanayamahime no Kami}}
* Sugawara Shrine
''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Sarutahiko Shrine
''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}

; Subordinate Shrines
* Inari Shrine
''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kodama Shrine
''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine
* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takeminakata}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

; Auxiliary Shrines
* Shinmei Shrine
''' {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}

''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kodama Shrine
''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. '''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main Shrine
* {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}
* {{ill|Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja|武渟河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Son of Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.
* {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建南方富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takeminakata}} - Enshrined from {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}.

; Auxiliary Shrines
* Shinmei Shrine
''' {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* Mizunami Shrine
''' {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Akiba Shrine
''' {{ill|Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja|火彦霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi no Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Ōyama-tsumi Shrine
''' {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ltt=Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
* Suminawa Shrine
''' {{ill|Hikosashiri no Kami|ja|彦狭知大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''' {{ill|Teojihori no Kami|ja|手置帆負大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teojihori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''' Spirit of the Hida Master Craftsman
* Tsushima Shrine
''' {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kōzan Shrine
''' {{ill|Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja|金山彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490520|1=Kanayamahiko}}
''' {{ill|Kanayamahime no Kami|ja|金山姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490521|1=Kanayamahime no Kami}}
* Sugawara Shrine
''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
''' {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
''' {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Sarutahiko Shrine
''' {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}

; Subordinate Shrines
* Inari Shrine
''' {{ill|Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja|宇賀御霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka no Mitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kodama Shrine
''' {{ill|Kukunochi no Kami|ja|久久能智大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.
