It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

'''Ōtsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamioka|ja|神岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamioka|1=Q11589576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589576}}, [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a prefectural shrine, and a special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}}.

== History ==
The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}} and the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} suggest that rituals were being conducted at the shrine during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.

During the {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} era, the local lord {{ill|Ema Clan|ja|江馬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema Clan|1=Q11551408|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551408}} invited a divided spirit of the {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin|ja|諏訪大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, changing the shrine's name to Suwa Daimyōjin. Following periods of rule by the Ema, {{ill|Miki Clan|ja|三木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki Clan|1=Q11355670|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355670}}, and {{ill|Kanamori Clan|ja|金森氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kanamori clan|WD=Q2975288}}, the shrine's original name was restored in 1804 after extensive research by followers of {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|WD=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}.

In 1871, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, Ōtsu Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. In 1930, it was elevated to special {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} status.

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Auxiliary Shrines
* Handwashing Basin
* First Torii Gate (completed in 1928)
* Second Torii Gate

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>

== Festivals ==
=== Kamioka Festival ===
The collective term for the annual festivals of Ōtsu Shrine, {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Hida City)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579587}}, celebrated on the same day. It is one of the "Three Great Festivals of Hida," alongside the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}} and {{ill|Furukawa Festival|ja|古川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Festival|1=Furukawa Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411179}}.

=== Funatsu Bon Odori ===
Held annually from August 14 to 16 in the shrine precincts. It was designated as an intangible cultural property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in 1969.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/funatubonn.html |title=Funatsu Bon Odori |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>
